$schema_note: Eval-case definitions for rai-orm-from-schema, v0.1. Format is intentionally simple — Claude reads this in-session, runs each case against the skill, and compares the output. Phase 4 stretch goal S6 promotes this to a Python-driven runner.
v0_1_pass_bar: E1 must pass on Northwind + TPC-H + synthetic; E2 coverage targets met per category per schema; E3 and E4 pass on all synthetic test cases; E5 pass with at least one stakeholder.

cases.description: Run the SRP against Northwind DDL; diff output against the reviewed reference solution.
pass_criteria: Object types match by name (modulo case): all 14 entity types + the OrderDetail objectified entity present.
Fact types match by structural shape: roles in same order; reading wording may differ.
Explicit-source constraints match exactly in type, target, and provenance.
Inferred constraints judged against the correctness rubric (see PLAN.md / Activity 1 / Constraint correctness rubric).

Antipattern flags surfaced for: denormalized address on customers/employees/orders.ship_*; ambiguous junctions on order_details, employee_territories, customer_customer_demo; boolean-encoded products.discontinued; self-FK ring on employees.reports_to.
equivalence_rules: reading wording can differ; semantic role-set must match

cases.description: Run the SRP against TPC-H DDL (with the spec-derived PK/FK augmentation); diff output against the reviewed reference solution.
pass_criteria: All 8 base entity types + 2 objectified entities (PartSupp, LineItem) present.
Composite PKs on PARTSUPP and LINEITEM emitted as external UCs preferred=true.
Composite FK from LINEITEM → PARTSUPP modelled as single binary fact type with composite-fk warning.
ZERO antipattern flags. TPC-H is the clean-baseline benchmark — false-positive antipatterns are a hard-fail.
Common-sense range proposals (Money/Count library entries) emitted; account-balance proposals correctly rejected.

cases.description: Run the SRP against the hand-built synthetic schema; diff output against the reviewed reference solution.
pass_criteria: All 21 object types present (including the 3 TYPE-column subtypes derived from PRODUCT.TYPE).
Subtype-partition constraint emitted with exclusive=true, exhaustive=true; product partition correctly identifies the three side tables.
All 6 encoded-enum antipatterns flagged: ORDERS.STATUS, SHIPMENT_EVENT.EVENT_TYPE, RETURN_REQUEST.REASON, PAYMENT.METHOD, CUSTOMER.TIER, PRODUCT.TYPE.
Both ambiguous-junction antipatterns surfaced: ORDER_ITEM_TAG (no extras → pure m:n default) and ORDER_PROMOTION (with extras → objectified default).
Common-sense ring constraints proposed for PRODUCT_CATEGORY parent (acyclic + asymmetric + irreflexive) and INVENTORY_TRANSFER from/to (irreflexive).
LLM-inferred subset constraint proposed for RETURN_REQUEST → DELIVERED orders, with rationale_world_fact present.
Common-sense temporal value-comparison constraints proposed for ORDERS placed/shipped/delivered and PROMOTION_CODE valid_from/valid_to.

cases.description: Count constraints by source category per schema; verify each category meets its expected target.

ddl-file.explicit: ≥ 80% of expected count (PKs, FKs, NOT NULLs, UNIQUEs)
ddl-file.common-sense: ≥ 60% of expected count (library hits + ring patterns)
ddl-file.llm-inferred: ≥ 1 proposal per schema if applicable, with rationale_world_fact populated

sql.sample: ≥ 50% of expected count (live data probing for value enums + ranges)

csv.sample: ≥ 80% of expected count (type inference + value enum + range from data)

pass_criteria: Per-category counts within tolerance for the input kind in use
Hard-fail if any input produces 0 explicit-source constraints when DDL is present

cases.description: Run the SRP with one column hidden from the schema; output must reflect the absence (correct partial model + flagged ambiguity), not silently invent.

test_variants.modification: Remove the `reports_to` column from `employees` and the corresponding ALTER TABLE FK statement before running the SRP.
test_variants.expected_behavior: Self-referential ring fact type employee_reports_to_manager NOT emitted; no ring constraints proposed. The Employee entity stays valid; no fabricated relationship.

test_variants.modification: Remove the `TYPE` column from `PRODUCT` before running the SRP.
test_variants.expected_behavior: TYPE-column subtype split NOT detected. PhysicalProduct/DigitalProduct/SubscriptionProduct subtypes NOT emitted. The three *_PRODUCT_DETAILS tables become regular entity types referencing Product with a `provenance.warning: dangling-side-table` flag rather than subtype rules.

test_variants.modification: Remove the `STATUS` column from `ORDERS` before running the SRP.
test_variants.expected_behavior: encoded-enum-in-varchar antipattern NOT flagged on ORDERS. The fact type orders_has_status NOT emitted. The OrderStatus value type NOT emitted. Subset constraint RETURN_REQUEST → DELIVERED orders NOT proposed (target column absent).

pass_criteria: Each variant produces a partial model with no fabricated entities, fact types, or constraints around the removed column.
The SRP does not silently fail or crash; missing-column scenarios surface as `provenance.warning` flags or are simply absent (without inventing replacements).

cases.description: Synthetic fixtures with planted antipatterns; output must flag each.

test_variants.pass: Both targets carry `provenance.warning: denormalized-address` with matched_columns listed.
All 6 columns flagged; sample-derived `allowed:` value lists match the data.
Both flagged with the corresponding default resolution.
Subtypes Car, Truck, Motorcycle (or similar) emitted with `extends=[Product]`; subtype-partition constraint emitted with exclusive=true, exhaustive=true; flag links to the three side tables.
Zero antipattern flags. The composite-fk informational marker on LINEITEM→PARTSUPP is permitted (it's not an antipattern; it's a structural note).

running_evals.manual_methodology: Claude runs the SRP on each fixture in-session, writes outputs to evals/results/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<schema>/{output.orm.yaml, output.py, output.verbalization.txt, diff.md}, then produces a per-case verdict by comparing against the expected file and the pass criteria.
running_evals.diff_format: diff.md per run records: (1) E1 structural diff (entity/fact-type set differences), (2) E2 source-category counts vs targets, (3) E3 degradation per variant, (4) E4 antipattern verdict per code, (5) overall pass/fail.
running_evals.automation_status: v0.1: manual. Phase 4 stretch goal S6 (eval runner) automates this if N grows.
